description: The diagram illustrates a multi-layered pipeline architecture for a decision-making process, structured into three main layers: Memory, Intelligence, and Experience. The pipeline stages are divided into four key phases: Sourcing (inbound), Screening (3-axis scoring), Due Diligence (truth-gap check), and Decision (matrix + score). The Memory layer includes a structured knowledge base with foundational data such as founders, decks, and signals, which is timestamped, deduplicated, and source-tagged for persistence. The Intelligence layer processes this data through a thesis engine (fund-specific filter) to generate a multi-axis score (3 axes + trend) and a trust score (evidence and flags). The Experience layer presents this processed information to the investor via a dashboard that ranks lists and momentum trends, as well as decision-ready outputs like memos and adversarial views. This architecture supports a systematic approach to sourcing, evaluating, and making informed decisions based on structured data and real-time insights.
